name: Addis Invoice OCR
description: AI-powered invoice processing pipeline for small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Users submit invoices (PDF/JPG/PNG) via a React web dashboard, Telegram bot, or WhatsApp. n8n orchestrates Google Document AI for OCR text extraction and DeepSeek for structured data parsing. Extracted data (vendor, date, line items, grand total, TIN, FS_No, subtotal, VAT) is persisted to Supabase (with Row-Level Security) and Google Sheets as a fallback, then surfaced in a searchable, filterable invoice history table with detail modals and PDF export. Multi-tenant isolation via Supabase Auth: anonymous sign-in on first visit provisions a secure JWT-backed session; RLS policies scope all database queries to the authenticated user — no localStorage UUIDs or manual tenant filtering needed. Authenticated routes (/upload, /invoices, /upgrade) are gated behind ProtectedRoute. Users can optionally upgrade to email/password accounts via Login/Signup pages, preserving their anonymous invoice history. Enforces a dual-layer 3-upload limit: a frontend soft-guard disables the upload zone, and a backend hard-guard short-circuits with HTTP 403 to protect AI token consumption. Full dark/light theme support and bilingual Amharic/English UI with ~250 translation keys.

frontend.name: addis-invoice-dashboard

branding.logo: /Alogo.png — custom logo image used in Navbar, footer, favicon, and landing page hero. Navbar also shows a gradient 'A' monogram fallback.

theming.dark_mode: Full dark/light theme via ThemeProvider context + Tailwind 'class' strategy. Respects prefers-color-scheme on first visit, persists choice in localStorage key 'addis_invoice_theme'. CSS custom properties define light/dark palettes for all surfaces, text, borders, and accents.
theming.bilingual: Amharic/English toggle via LanguageProvider context (~250 translation keys in i18n/translations.ts). Persists choice in localStorage key 'addis_invoice_lang'. Applies 'lang-am' class to <html> for Amharic-specific CSS overrides (line-height, text transforms, column min-widths). LanguageToggle button in Navbar shows active language in cyan.

auth.provider: Supabase Auth — anonymous sign-in on first visit provisions a JWT-backed session with a persistent user ID. RLS (Row-Level Security) policies on the 'invoices' table automatically scope all SELECT/INSERT queries to the authenticated user, eliminating the need for manual user_id filtering in application code.
auth.flow: On app mount, AuthProvider attempts to restore an existing session via supabase.auth.getSession(). If no session exists, it calls supabase.auth.signInAnonymously() to provision a new anonymous user silently. The onAuthStateChange listener keeps session state in sync. useAuth() hook exposes user, session, isLoading, isAnonymous, and signOut to the component tree.
auth.protected_routes: ProtectedRoute wrapper checks useAuth().user — if null (not authenticated) and loading is complete, redirects to /login. Covers /upload, /invoices, and /upgrade. Shows a centered spinner while the session is being restored from storage.
auth.account_upgrade: Signup page handles two paths: (1) anonymous user → calls supabase.auth.updateUser() to attach email/password, preserving the existing user.id and all previously uploaded invoices; (2) fresh user → calls supabase.auth.signUp() to create a new account. Login page calls supabase.auth.signInWithPassword(). Both redirect to /upload on success.

pages./: Landing page with hero (interactive radial-glow background that tracks cursor), how-it-works steps (3-step flow: ScanSearch → Bot → Landmark), sample invoice result card (Abebe Trading PLC demo with TIN, FS_No, subtotal, total), pricing tiers (Starter 300ETB/50 invoices, Pro 750ETB/500 invoices, Enterprise Custom), features grid, FAQ accordion (data safety, Amharic support, cancellation), and WhatsApp-driven CTA sections — all styled in cyan (#06B6D4) theme
pages./login: Email/password login form with Supabase Auth signInWithPassword. Gradient 'A' monogram header, Email + Password fields with Lucide icons, error display, loading spinner on submit, and link to /signup for new users. Redirects to /upload on success.
pages./signup: Email/password registration form. Two paths: anonymous account upgrade (updateUser — preserves existing user.id and invoice history) or fresh sign-up (signUp). Same visual style as Login with UserPlus icon. Password minimum 6 characters. Links to /login for existing users.
pages./upload: Drag-and-drop invoice upload with free trial status bar (count / 3). Three-state limit guard: loading enables upload, error enables upload (fail-open), count ≥3 disables upload with premium upgrade card (Crown badge + WhatsApp CTA). Processing UI with progress bar and three-step checklist (upload, read/layout, prepare/tax). Result card (ResultsCard) on completion, detail modal (DetailsModal), and 'View Latest Invoice' button. Wrapped in ProtectedRoute.
pages./invoices: Searchable, filterable, sortable invoice history table (InvoiceTable) with 9 columns: Date, Vendor, Tax IDs (TIN + FS_No badges), Subtotal, VAT, Grand Total, Items, Source (cyan badge), Actions. Detail modal (DetailsModal) shows tax breakdown: Subtotal, VAT, Grand Total. PDF download via jsPDF. Client-side pagination (10 per page). Fetches from Supabase (RLS-scoped). Wrapped in ProtectedRoute.
pages./upgrade: Premium upgrade page with Telebirr payment flow. User uploads a payment screenshot (drag-and-drop with file preview), attaches their phone number (09XXXXXXXX), and submits for manual verification. Payment amount: 750 ETB. States: idle → uploading screenshot → submitted. Wrapped in ProtectedRoute.

layout.shell: App shell layout: sticky Navbar (top), collapsible SideDock (left, 270px expanded / 76px collapsed, hidden on mobile), <main> content area (lg:pl-[300px]) with dot-grid background, footer with logo + quick links + Telegram/WhatsApp support links, and FloatingSupport FAB (fixed bottom-right).
layout.side_dock: SideDock — fixed left sidebar visible on lg+ screens. Collapsible with smooth 200ms transition. Three grouped nav sections (Menu, Learn More, Get Started) from navGroupDefs config. Active route indicator (cyan left-border bar). Items have staggered entry animations on expand. Uses NavLink for internal routes, <a> for external links, <button> for hash-section scrolling.
layout.nav_groups: navGroupDefs in lib/navigation.ts defines 3 groups with typed nav items: route (NavLink), hash (scrollToSection), external (<a>). Each item has a Lucide icon. Menu group: Home, Upload Invoice, Invoices, Upgrade. Learn More group: How it Works, Pricing, Features, FAQ. Get Started group: Free Trial (WhatsApp), Telegram Support, WhatsApp Business. scrollToSection handles route-aware hash scrolling (navigates to / then scrolls if not already on home).

components: AuthProvider — React context for Supabase Auth session management. Restores existing session on mount, silently provisions anonymous sign-in for new visitors, listens to onAuthStateChange. Exposes useAuth() hook returning user, session, isLoading, isAnonymous, signOut. Wraps the entire app tree in App.tsx.

Navbar — sticky top nav (z-40) with gradient 'A' monogram + 'Addis Invoice' text logo. Right-side controls: LanguageToggle (EN/AM), dark/light ThemeToggle, and hamburger menu for mobile. Mobile menu renders all three nav groups from navGroupDefs.

SideDock — fixed left sidebar (z-30, hidden on mobile, visible on lg+). Collapsible: 270px expanded, 76px collapsed. Three grouped nav sections from navGroupDefs. Active route indicator (cyan left-border bar). Staggered item entry animations. Uses NavLink for routes, <a> for external links (Telegram Support, WhatsApp Business), <button> for hash navigation (How it Works, Pricing, Features, FAQ).

FloatingSupport — fixed bottom-right floating action button (FAB, z-50) with headset icon and subtle pulse ring animation. Expands on click to reveal two channel picker links: Telegram Support (t.me/AddisInvoiceSupportBot) and WhatsApp Business (wa.me/251701681571). Staggered menu item animations. Closes on outside click or Escape.

LanguageProvider — React context for bilingual Amharic/English support. Provides t() lookup function (~250 keys), language state, setLanguage, toggleLanguage, isAmharic boolean, and textClass helper ('i18n-am' class). Persists to localStorage key 'addis_invoice_lang'. Sets 'lang-am' class on <html>.
LanguageToggle — EN / AM toggle button in Navbar. Active language highlighted in cyan, inactive in gray. Calls toggleLanguage from LanguageProvider context.
ThemeProvider — React context for dark/light theme. Toggles 'dark' class on <html>. Persists choice to localStorage key 'addis_invoice_theme'. Initial value respects prefers-color-scheme media query.

UploadZone — drag-and-drop file upload area with type/size validation and visual drag-over feedback (glow ring animation). Accepts disabled prop for limit guard — when disabled, applies opacity-60 + pointer-events-none. Shows file format badges (PDF, JPG/PNG) and max size label (10 MB). Hidden file input triggered by click.

ProcessingSpinner — simple standalone spinning loader with cyan border animation. Available as a reusable component; Upload.tsx uses its own inline processing UI with progress bar and three-step checklist instead.

ResultsCard — post-processing result card. Shows extracted data: line items table (Item, Qty, Unit Price, Total) with row count, items summary box (if present), and specification sheet (Vendor, Invoice Date, Grand Total). Action buttons: View Invoice (cyan primary), Save Invoice, Download PDF, Upload Another. Currency formatted in ETB via Intl.NumberFormat.
FilterBar — search input (with Search icon), From/To date inputs, Clear button, and 'Showing X of Y invoices' count display. White card with rounded-2xl border and cyan focus rings.

InvoiceTable — paginated (10/page), sortable by Date/Vendor/Amount columns with arrow indicators. 9 columns: Date, Vendor, Tax IDs (TIN + FS_No badges), Subtotal, VAT, Grand Total, Items, Source (cyan badge), Actions (view, download PDF, delete with confirm dialog). Zebra-striped rows. Empty state with Inbox icon. Skeleton loading rendered inline in Invoices.tsx (9 columns, 5 rows, shimmer animation).

DetailsModal — full-screen overlay (z-100, bg-black/60 backdrop) with pop-in animation. Two-column layout: Vendor name + Grand Total large display. Info grid: Date, Source, TIN, FS_No, Subtotal, VAT, Items count. Line items table with grand total in footer. Items summary shown if present. Sticky footer: Download PDF (cyan primary) + Close buttons. ESC key closes.
SectionHeading — internal component in Home.tsx: centered heading with cyan accent bar, label badge, title, and description. Used by all landing page sections.
FAQItem — internal component in Home.tsx: disclosure (expand/collapse) accordion item with smooth height transition. Used in the FAQ section.

ProtectedRoute — route guard wrapper. Checks useAuth() for authenticated user; redirects to /login if null. Shows centered loading spinner while session is being restored from Supabase. Used on /upload, /invoices, and /upgrade routes.

hooks.useInvoiceUpload: Manages upload lifecycle state machine: idle → uploading → processing → uploaded/completed/failed. Posts multipart FormData (fields: file) to n8n POST webhook (path: 8a102812-cb84-422b-b168-a20f9de68f4b) via centralized Axios client. Attaches Supabase JWT as Bearer Authorization header. Handles upload progress (Axios onUploadProgress), 150s timeout, AbortController cleanup, React StrictMode remount safety (mountedRef), response classification (OCR acknowledgment vs legacy full invoice), and granular error classification (timeout, network, 413, 404, generic). On successful invoice extraction, persists the result to Supabase via saveInvoiceToSupabase() — fetches the current session, extracts session.user.id, and includes it as user_id in the INSERT so RLS WITH CHECK policies match auth.uid().
hooks.useInvoiceList: Fetches invoice history from Supabase (RLS automatically scopes to the authenticated user — no explicit user_id filter needed). Selects all columns, orders by date descending, caps at 15 most recent rows. Normalizes raw database rows via normalizeInvoice() mapping fields including tax data: tin/TIN, fs_no/FS_No, subtotal/Subtotal, vat_amount/VAT_Amount/VAT. Provides search (vendor + items_summary), date range filtering, sorting (date/vendor/amount, asc/desc), refetch, and totalCount for limit guard. Delete performs a real Supabase deletion (supabase.from('invoices').delete().eq('id', id)) with success/error toast feedback and automatic refetch — no more local-only hide.

lib.api: Centralized Axios instance (baseURL from VITE_API_URL env var, fallback http://localhost:5678/webhook/, default 30s timeout). Used exclusively for the OCR upload pipeline (POST multipart/form-data). Invoice history retrieval goes directly through Supabase.
lib.supabaseClient: Supabase client singleton (createClient from @supabase/supabase-js). Configured via VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY env vars. Gracefully warns if env vars are missing but creates the client anyway so the React tree renders without crashing. Used by AuthProvider (auth), useInvoiceList (data reads), and useInvoiceUpload (data writes).
lib.multi_tenant: Multi-tenant isolation via Supabase Row-Level Security (RLS) with three active policies on the 'invoices' table: SELECT (USING auth.uid() = user_id), INSERT (WITH CHECK auth.uid() = user_id), DELETE (USING auth.uid() = user_id). Every visitor gets an anonymous Supabase Auth session on first visit (JWT-backed, persistent user ID). The database itself enforces partitioning — no manual user_id filters in application code. The useInvoiceUpload hook explicitly sets user_id from session.user.id on insert so RLS WITH CHECK passes. The n8n webhook receives a Bearer token from the upload request for server-side identity verification. Users who sign up with email/password upgrade their anonymous account via updateUser(), preserving all existing invoice history under the same user.id.
lib.i18n: Complete bilingual dictionary (~250 translation keys) in i18n/translations.ts covering nav, hero, how-it-works, pricing, features, FAQ, CTA, upload, table, invoices, filter, results, modal, auth (login/signup), upgrade, support, footer, and error states. Exports interpolate() helper for template strings like '{count}'. All UI text rendered via t('key') from LanguageProvider context.
lib.navigation: navGroupDefs in navigation.ts defines 3 grouped nav sections (Menu, Learn More, Get Started) with typed items (route/hash/external), each with a Lucide icon. Menu group includes the /upgrade route. Exports scrollToSection (hash scroll with route-aware behavior — navigates to / then scrolls if not on home) and scrollToHash (in-page scroll).

environments.development: VITE_API_URL=https://n8n.magnetai.cc/webhook/ in .env.development (currently points to production — same as .env.production)
environments.supabase: VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in .env and .env.production (both files include Supabase credentials so Cloudflare Pages builds have them available)

OCR-3.3-web.description: Main invoice processing workflow with three entry points: Web upload (POST multipart/form-data), Telegram bot, and WhatsApp. Supports multi-tenant verification via Bearer token from Supabase Auth. Enforces backend hard-guard: queries tracking sheet immediately after file trigger; if user row count ≥3, short-circuits with HTTP 403 before reaching OCR/AI nodes.

web_path.trigger: POST /webhook/8a102812-cb84-422b-b168-a20f9de68f4b (multipart/form-data with fields: file; Authorization: Bearer <supabase_jwt>)
flow: 1. Webhook receives file upload + Bearer token from frontend
2. Hard-Guard Quota Check — queries Google Sheets tracking sheet for existing rows matching this user; if count ≥3, returns HTTP 403 immediately to protect DeepSeek token budget
3. Generate Job ID — creates job_id, status=queued, passes binary file forward

4. Respond to Webhook — immediately returns {"status":"success","message":"File uploaded successfully! Your invoice is being processed in the background."} with CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *)
5. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR text extraction (australia-southeast1 region)
6. Switch PDF/Image — routes to appropriate HTTP Request node based on detected file type
7. AI Agent + DeepSeek Chat Model (deepseek-v4-flash) + Structured Output Parser — parses vendor_name, date, grand_total, items_summary, is_invoice from extracted OCR text
8. Edit Fields — normalizes and formats extracted data
9. If valid invoice (is_invoice=true) → Append or update row in Google Sheets (fallback persistence)
10. If not valid (is_invoice=false) → Send rejection message (Telegram path only)
11. Switch → Send Telegram/WhatsApp success notification to user

telegram_path.trigger: Telegram bot receives invoice file message
flow: 1. Telegram Trigger — listens for document/photo messages
2. Get a file — downloads file binary from Telegram servers
3. Extract from File — Google Document AI OCR text extraction
4. Switch PDF/Image → HTTP Request to Document AI processor
5. Converges with web path at AI Agent node for DeepSeek parsing and Sheets storage

whatsapp_path.flow: WhatsApp integration — receives invoice files via WhatsApp Business API and feeds into the shared OCR+AI pipeline

ai_models.ocr: Google Document AI — australia-southeast1 region, processor for PDF/image OCR text extraction
ai_models.parsing: DeepSeek Chat Model — deepseek-v4-flash via n8n LangChain nodes with Structured Output Parser

structured_output_schema.is_invoice: boolean — true if content is a valid invoice/receipt, false for unrelated images/files
structured_output_schema.date: string — invoice date extracted from document (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.vendor_name: string — standardized vendor/business name, Amharic names translated to English (empty if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.grand_total: number — final total amount due on the invoice (0 if not an invoice)
structured_output_schema.items_summary: string — concise text summary of purchased items/services (empty if not an invoice)

multi_tenant.primary: Supabase Auth + RLS — the frontend provisions anonymous JWT sessions; the 'invoices' table enforces row-level security via auth.uid(). The frontend attaches the Supabase access token as a Bearer Authorization header on upload requests for server-side identity verification.
multi_tenant.google_sheets_fallback: The n8n workflow also writes to Google Sheets (column 'User_ID') as a fallback data store. The privacy fix ensures the Google Sheets connector node filters rows by User_ID at the connector layer before payload transmission.
multi_tenant.note: The legacy localStorage-based UUID system (crypto.randomUUID() stored in 'addis_invoice_user_id') has been fully replaced by Supabase Auth.

upload_limit_guard.frontend_soft_guard: Upload.tsx calls useInvoiceList hook on mount to get totalCount. When totalCount ≥3: UploadZone receives disabled={true} (opacity-60, pointer-events-none, file input disabled). An amber card displays 'Upload Limit Reached (3/3) — Upgrade to Premium' with a WhatsApp CTA link. Fails open: if the history check is loading or errors, the upload zone remains enabled.
upload_limit_guard.backend_hard_guard: Immediately after the webhook trigger receives the file, a quota check node queries the Google Sheets tracking sheet for rows matching the incoming user. If the match count is ≥3, the workflow short-circuits — it returns HTTP 403 (Forbidden) to the client and stops execution before reaching the Google Document AI OCR node or the DeepSeek Chat Model, protecting expensive AI token consumption.

immediate_ack.message: File uploaded successfully! Your invoice is being processed in the background.

hard_limit_403.message: Upload limit reached. You have used all 3 free uploads. Upgrade to Premium to continue.

response_format.async_result: Written to Google Sheets (with User_ID column) as fallback; primary persistence is Supabase via the frontend hook

get-invoices.description: GET webhook that reads rows from Google Sheets filtered by User_ID at the connector node layer, takes the 15 most recent, reverses them (newest first), and returns as a JSON array with normalized field names including tax fields (TIN, FS_No, Subtotal, VAT_Amount). NOTE: This endpoint is now a fallback — the primary invoice history source is Supabase (RLS-scoped, queried directly by useInvoiceList).
flow: 1. Webhook receives GET request with ?userId=<UUID> query param
2. Google Sheets node — reads rows from Sheet, filters by User_ID column at connector level (no cross-tenant data transmitted)

3. Code node — slices last 15 rows, reverses order (newest first), normalizes field names: vendor, date, grand_total (from Total Amount), items_summary (from Summary), source, id, tin (TIN), fs_no (FS_No), subtotal (Subtotal), vat_amount (VAT_Amount)
4. Returns JSON array to client with CORS headers
get-invoices.status: Legacy fallback — Supabase is the primary invoice history data source
response_fields: items_summary — Summary text of purchased items

primary.security: Row-Level Security (RLS) enforced via 3 policies: SELECT (USING auth.uid() = user_id), INSERT (WITH CHECK auth.uid() = user_id), DELETE (USING auth.uid() = user_id). Each authenticated user can only access their own rows. No manual user_id filtering required in application code.

fallback.privacy: Rows are filtered by User_ID at the Google Sheets connector node layer — the filter is applied server-side before data leaves Google's infrastructure.

data_flow.auth: Visitor opens app → AuthProvider restores existing Supabase session or silently provisions anonymous sign-in → JWT-backed session with persistent user.id → RLS policies scope all DB queries to this user → Optional account upgrade via Signup page (updateUser preserves user.id and invoice history) or Login page (signInWithPassword)
data_flow.upload: User submits file via Web (drag-and-drop at /upload, ProtectedRoute gated) / Telegram bot / WhatsApp → Frontend attaches Supabase JWT as Bearer Authorization header → POST to n8n webhook (8a102812-cb84-422b-b168-a20f9de68f4b) → Backend hard-guard quota check (≥3 rows for this user → 403 short-circuit) → n8n acknowledges immediately with {"status":"success"} → Background pipeline: Google Document AI OCR → DeepSeek v4-flash AI parsing (vendor, date, total, items, TIN, FS_No, subtotal, VAT) → Append to Google Sheets (fallback) → Telegram/WhatsApp notification to user → Frontend hook persists result to Supabase with user_id set from session (RLS WITH CHECK passes)
data_flow.history: Frontend useInvoiceList queries Supabase 'invoices' table (RLS auto-scopes to auth.uid()) → orders by date descending, limits to 15 rows → normalizes fields (tin, fs_no, subtotal, vat_amount, etc.) → Frontend InvoiceTable renders 9-column sortable table with TIN, FS_No, Subtotal, VAT, Grand Total. Delete performs real Supabase deletion with toast feedback and automatic refetch.
data_flow.detail_view: User clicks View Invoice (eye icon in table or button on ResultsCard) → DetailsModal overlay opens with full invoice data: vendor, date, TIN, FS_No, subtotal, VAT, grand total, line items table, items summary → Download PDF button generates formatted PDF via jsPDF + jspdf-autotable

channels.web: React dashboard at /upload (drag-and-drop with 3-upload limit guard, ProtectedRoute gated) and /invoices (searchable/filterable 9-column history table with tax breakdown, ProtectedRoute gated)

telegram.support_bot: https://t.me/AddisInvoiceSupportBot — handles scaled user ticketing. Inbound messages are routed directly to the private Admin Operations Group (chat ID: -1003690948102) for centralized support team triage and response.
telegram.invoice_bot: Telegram bot accepting invoice file submissions and returning extracted summaries. Integrated into the shared OCR-3.3 workflow pipeline.

whatsapp.usage: WhatsApp Business channel for: invoice file submission, free-trial signups (7-day trial with pre-filled message), enterprise inquiries, and premium upgrade flow from the upload limit guard CTA.
whatsapp.landing_page: Home page hero CTA ('Try Free for 7 Days'), all pricing tier CTAs, and footer CTA ('Message us on WhatsApp') link to wa.me/251701681571 with context-specific pre-filled messages.

channels.floating_widget: FloatingSupport FAB (fixed bottom-right, z-50) presents a headset icon with slow-pulse ring. On click, expands two channel buttons: 'Telegram Support' (t.me/AddisInvoiceSupportBot, Telegram-blue hover) and 'WhatsApp Business' (wa.me/251701681571, WhatsApp-green hover). Staggered menu item animations. Present on all pages.

pricing.trial: 7-day free trial via WhatsApp — no credit card required

pro_upgrade.method: Telebirr — user uploads payment screenshot on /upgrade page with phone number, submitted for manual verification
pro_upgrade.page: /upgrade — ProtectedRoute gated, drag-and-drop screenshot upload, phone number input, submission confirmation

key_fixes_applied: Removed manual Content-Type: multipart/form-data header — let Axios set boundary automatically (prevents malformed multipart requests)
Fixed React StrictMode double-mount bug — mountedRef not reset to true on remount, silently dropping all upload state callbacks
Added 'View Invoice' button to upload result screen — fetches latest invoice from API and opens DetailsModal
Centralized API configuration in lib/api.ts with VITE_API_URL env var and localhost fallback
Separated dev/prod environments with single VITE_API_URL in .env files
Redesigned homepage with landing page sections (hero, how-it-works, pricing, features, FAQ, footer CTA) and cyan (#06B6D4) color theme
Rebranded all Ledger references to Addis Invoice with Alogo.png custom logo across Navbar, footer, and favicon

Migrated multi-tenant system from localStorage-based UUID to Supabase Auth: anonymous sign-in provisions JWT-backed sessions, RLS policies scope all DB queries to auth.uid(), removing the need for manual user_id filtering in application code
Added AuthProvider component wrapping the app tree — handles session restoration, anonymous sign-in, and onAuthStateChange listener
Added ProtectedRoute wrapper on /upload, /invoices, and /upgrade — redirects unauthenticated users to /login with loading spinner
Added Login page (email/password sign-in via Supabase signInWithPassword) and Signup page (anonymous upgrade via updateUser preserving invoice history, or fresh sign-up via signUp)
Added /upgrade page with Telebirr payment screenshot upload and phone number submission flow

Integrated Supabase as primary data store: useInvoiceList fetches via Supabase (RLS-scoped), useInvoiceUpload persists results to Supabase after OCR with explicit user_id from session, supabaseClient singleton configured via VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY env vars

Created 3 RLS policies on the invoices table: SELECT (USING auth.uid() = user_id), INSERT (WITH CHECK auth.uid() = user_id), DELETE (USING auth.uid() = user_id) — the deny-all default was blocking all frontend queries
Fixed useInvoiceUpload saveInvoiceToSupabase() to explicitly fetch session.user.id and include it as user_id in the INSERT, so RLS WITH CHECK policies pass correctly
Upgraded useInvoiceList deleteInvoice from local-only hide to real Supabase deletion: supabase.from('invoices').delete().eq('id', invoiceId) with success/error toast feedback and automatic refetch
Added TIN, FS_No, Subtotal, and VAT_Amount fields to InvoiceData type, invoice history table (InvoiceTable), detail modal (DetailsModal), PDF export, and Supabase/API response normalization
Fixed Google Sheets privacy leak: connector node now filters rows by User_ID column at the database layer BEFORE payload transmission — cross-tenant data no longer traverses the network

Implemented dual-layer 3-upload limit guard: frontend soft-guard disables UploadZone at count ≥3 with upgrade CTA; backend hard-guard short-circuits pipeline with HTTP 403 before consuming OCR/AI tokens
Added FloatingSupport FAB widget (fixed bottom-right) with Telegram Support Bot and WhatsApp Business channel picker — staggered menu animations, closes on outside click or Escape
Updated all WhatsApp links across landing page, pricing CTAs, footer, and upload limit guard to use WhatsApp Business number 251701681571
Configured Telegram Support Bot (t.me/AddisInvoiceSupportBot) routing inbound messages to private Admin Operations Group (-1003690948102) for centralized ticket management
Implemented full dark/light theme support via ThemeProvider context + CSS custom properties — respects prefers-color-scheme, persists choice to localStorage

Implemented bilingual Amharic/English UI (~250 translation keys) via LanguageProvider context + LanguageToggle button in Navbar — persists choice to localStorage, Amharic CSS overrides via lang-am class
Added collapsible SideDock left sidebar (270px/76px, lg+ only) with three grouped nav sections from navGroupDefs config, active route indicator, and staggered entry animations
Replaced react-icons usage with lucide-react for consistent icon styling across all components and navigation (react-icons remains in package.json as an unused residual dependency)
Added SectionHeading and FAQItem internal components to Home.tsx for consistent landing page section headers and accordion items
Added /upgrade route to navGroupDefs Menu section in navigation.ts
Scrubbed legacy n8n workflow JSON backup files from git history (OCR-2.0-web, OCR-3.3 whatsup, OCR-3.0-web dev, OCR-get-invoices dev) — repo is now workflow-file-free
target_users: Small businesses in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. Supports Amharic and English invoice formats. Multi-tenant architecture via Supabase Auth + RLS: each visitor gets an anonymous JWT session on first visit, database-level row security ensures complete data isolation. Users can optionally upgrade to email/password accounts while preserving their invoice history.

deployment.frontend: Vercel (build: npm run build, output: front/dist/, SPA routing via _redirects: /* /index.html 200)
deployment.database: Supabase (PostgreSQL with RLS, anonymous auth, invoices table)
